title: Devices and methods for therapeutic heat treatment
abstract: An example implantable microparticle for delivering therapeutic heat treatment comprises a generally spherical body. The body may be formed from a first material comprising a biodegradable material and a second material comprising a Curie temperature material. The biodegradable material may be a non-Curie temperature material or have a Curie temperature lower than a Curie temperature of the Curie temperature material. The first material and the second material are mixed to form a composite having a Curie temperature in the range of 35 Â°C and 100 Â°C.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An implantable microparticle comprising: a generally spherical body, the body comprising: a first material comprising a biodegradable material; a second material comprising a Curie temperature material; wherein the biodegradable material is a non-Curie temperature material or has a Curie temperature lower than a Curie temperature of the Curie temperature material; and wherein the first material and the second material are mixed to form a composite having a Curie temperature in the range of 35 Â°C and 100 Â°C. 2. The implantable microparticle of claim 1, wherein a ratio of first material to the second material is greater than 1 : 1. 3. The implantable microparticle of any one of claims 1-2, wherein the first material comprises a magnesium based compound. 4. The implantable microparticle of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the first material comprises magnesium oxide. 5. The implantable microparticle of any one of claims 1-2, wherein the first material comprises a biocompatible polymer. 6. The implantable microparticle of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the second material comprises an iron oxide. 7. The implantable microparticle of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the second material comprises an iron alloy. 8. The implantable microparticle of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the second material comprises a plurality of nanoparticles. 9. The implantable microparticle of claim 8, wherein the plurality of nanoparticles each have a particle size in the range of 0.1 to 2.5 nanometers. 10. The implantable microparticle of any one of claims 1-9, further comprising a therapeutic agent. 11. The implantable microparticle of claim 10, wherein the therapeutic agent is disposed on an outer surface of the spherical body. 12. The implantable microparticle of claim 10, wherein the therapeutic is intermixed with the first material and the second material. 13. The implantable microparticle of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the microparticle is configured to degrade over a period of time. 14. The implantable microparticle of claim 13, wherein the period of time is in the range of 3 to 6 months. 15. The implantable microparticle of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the microparticle has a diameter in the range of 1-3000 microns.
